title: Fitting a gravity overflow pipe to a cold water storage tank
subtitle: Install or replace a 22 mm overflow pipe on a loft cold water tank so it discharges safely outside if the ball valve fails
excerpt: How to fit an overflow pipe to a cold water storage cistern in the loft. Covers drilling the tank at the correct height, fitting the overflow connector, running the 22 mm pipe to an external discharge point, and checking the ball valve setting.
sourceNotes: UK plumbing trade practice. Water Supply (Water Fittings) Regulations 1999 (requirements for cistern overflows. Reader's Digest Complete DIY Manual (referenced, not reproduced).

glossaryTerms.definition: A plastic or galvanised-steel tank typically installed in the roof space of a UK house to store cold water at atmospheric pressure for distribution to the hot water cylinder and sometimes to bathroom cold supplies. Fitted with a ball valve (ballcock) that shuts off the incoming supply when the water level reaches the set height. If the ball valve fails open, the cistern will overflow) hence the mandatory 22 mm overflow pipe that discharges outside the building.

content.text: needs a 22 mm overflow pipe to the outside in case the ball valve fails open. Drill the tank at the right height, fit the overflow connector, run the pipe through the roof space to a visible discharge point, and check the ball valve level is set below the overflow hole.

The overflow inlet must be at least 25 mm below the bottom of the ball valve body and at least 25 mm above the tank rim to allow a safety margin. Mark the overflow hole position on the tank side, measuring these clearances from the ball valve position. The overflow hole is typically 22 to 25 mm diameter (use a hole saw or stepped drill.
Drill the tank and fit the connector

Drill the tank at the marked point. Most modern plastic cisterns have thin walls that cut cleanly with a 22 mm hole saw at low speed. Deburr the hole. Pass the tank connector through from the outside and thread the nut on from the inside. Tighten to finger-tight plus one quarter turn) do not overtighten plastic connectors. Do not use sealant on the gasket; compression alone seals.
Run the pipe to the outside

Run 22 mm plastic overflow pipe from the tank connector through the roof space and out through an exterior wall, typically under a soffit or through the gable end. The pipe must fall continuously from tank to discharge (no dips that would trap water. The discharge point should be visible from a window so an overflow is noticed. Fit a mesh guard on the external end to keep insects and birds from nesting.

With the overflow in place, check the ball valve shuts off before the water level reaches the overflow inlet. The ball arm angle sets this) bend or adjust to lower the water level if the tank fills above the overflow hole. Water should never reach the overflow under normal conditions; dripping from the overflow pipe means the ball valve needs attention.
